entry.title: Re: LE-WM using scan to count a Product during a TO confi...
entry.content:
Hello @Miguel_Nava 

That looks like a quite typical scenario. It's exactly the same way of working as at a counter at any store.

SAP standard RF transactions don't work this way. However you can replace standard RF screen and build own scanning logic with User Exit Screens 

I would not worry about performance as product scans in your scenario will not post any data. RF transaction needs just to count scans.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Create Customer Project via app 'Plan Customer Project'
entry.content:
Hi @nilkpmg ,

the message that shows up is a warning message you can skip and use all professional service functionalities also for projects with a duration longer than three years. If the project is running this long it can be set up in this way, however, a lot of projects have a shorter duration.

Best regards,

David

entry.title: Re: SAP Asset Accounting End Year Close
entry.content: thank you

entry.title: SAP Community Call for ALM – APJ edition
entry.content:
The monthly webinar - focusing on the APJ region and timezone - provides everything you need to know about SAP's Application Lifecycle Management (ALM) offerings. Get the latest news and regional updates directly from SAP's topic experts!

By attending this call you have the chance to stay updated about what's going on with SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Focused Run.

For slides and recordings click here. 

Link to register is coming soon!

entry.title: How to trigger Workflow in SAP Commerce when A Product is received from S/4
entry.content: Hi Everyone! We are working on integrating SAP Commerce cloud with S/4Hana. We have managed to get products from S/4 into SAP commerce catalog. Our aim is to trigger a workflow whenever a new product is imported into SAP Commerce from S/4Hana so that product manager is notified about new product and take the required action. We have managed to create a workflow with desired actions but are struggling with workflow trigger. We are aware of triggering a workflow with workflowProcessingService but We are unable to figure out how to determine a new product is imported from S/4Hana. Any leads on this will be helpful.

entry.title: Re: Taking Groups to the Next Level with Application-Specific Groups
entry.content:
Hi Ivelina,

 

is the also an option planned for the identities ? 

Our Usecase we need somehow filter which user are created on which ABAP Systems . We would not like to create every user on every abap system. So we need something like a filter on the target system that it will only sync/creat the needed users for the desired target system.

Our first tought we use the group membeship as filter , but we must test it if this can be used in the transformation as filter

entry.title: Error in Replication Flow in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing an error while replicating data from S4 HANA Public Cloud to SAP Datasphere for master data cds via replication flow. Attaching the error screenshot.

 

Note: The DSP tenant is freshly setup so it could be something to do with the connection as well, But we have a communication scenario,Arrangment and User in Place.

entry.content:
Hello Experts,

 

May I ask if you have encounter this error during uploading of Delivery via DTW. my DTW file is posted below.

TYIA

entry.title: Re: New Depreciation Area: Where are T093-VIEW0/1/2/3/4/5/6 configured?
entry.content: Hi Can any one Please assist. on this configuration of T093-VIEW0/1/2/3/4/5/6 should be in other transaction.

entry.title: Re: Taking Groups to the Next Level with Application-Specific Groups
entry.content: There is a lot of filters available, both as properties and you're free to customize it in your transformation. See help documentation ' list of properties'

entry.title: Re: Intelligent Invoice Scanning
entry.content:
Hi Kathy,

the screen is correct. Please use the Shift key instead, that should do the job. For me it worked. I selected the first line, scrolled down, selected the last line using Shift. Then all the lines in between got selected.

Best regards,

Stefan

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 Getting Started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA (Bangalore, India)
entry.content: @Vitaliy-R I'd love to! I am always keen to join and meet HANA ML users!

entry.title: Re: How to trigger Workflow in SAP Commerce when A Produc...
entry.content:
Hello @ketkimujumdar,
May I suggest as below which might help - 
- add a custom attribute (isNewFromS4Hana) to ProductModel and set true when new product got imported.
- implement an Interceptor (using ModelSaveInterceptor or PrepareInterceptor) to check if a product creatyed newly.
- or using ImpEx for product imports, utilize afterImportHook to detect new records and trigger a workflow.
If it helped, mark thread as accepted solution.
Thanks & Regards,
Kunal Bansal, SAP CX SME
https://www.linkedin.com/in/thekunalbansal

entry.title: Re: A Comprehensive Overview of Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM)
entry.content:
Hi,
Could you please help, is it possible with this application I can read emails and attachments and create posting in SAP S/4 HANA? we have requirement to post payment with help of remittance reading

entry.title: Re: 🔴 Filtering of RAP business events in SAP BTP ABAP Environment and in AEM
entry.content:
Hi Erik,

We receive the event from SAP AEM and then hand it over to your custom coding for the event handling. After we received and acknowledged the event, the event is removed from the AEM queues as from AEM's point of view, the event has been successfully delivered.

If you raise an exception during the event consumption, the event is set to status failed and moved to an internal dead-letter queue, I guess similar to DMQ on AEM side. This means you can manually re-trigger the event consumption or delete the failed event. It is not clear to me, why you want to put the event back on the AEM queue instead or what you want to achieve eventually?

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content:
Hi everyone, I'm Omar. My SAP journey began when I was job hunting outside Morocco. I discovered that many companies required SAP knowledge, which sparked my interest in this technology. My goal evolved from simply being an SAP user to becoming a consultant.

I completed an 8-month professional certificate course in SAP consulting on Coursera. This gave me a solid foundation in SAP, including understanding its structure and the process of solution implementation.

Now I'm looking for ways to gain real-world, practical experience. I'd love to hear any suggestions you have on how to bridge the gap between my training and real-world application.

entry.title: Through Product Viewer, can we point at a physical asset & see the details/ instructions?
entry.content: Using SAP Product Viewer, can we point at a physical asset & try to fetch the instructions & object part details? Currently we have only VR or AR. Can we load 3D model & view the parts/ instructions?

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content:
Hey Everyone 
I am Omar, from Morocco, from Casablanca, the financial heart of Africa 😝, happy to be in this Community

regards

Hi everyone, I'm Omar. My SAP journey began when I was job hunting outside Morocco. I discovered that many companies required SAP knowledge, which sparked my interest in this technology. My goal evolved from simply being an SAP user to becoming a consultant.

I completed an 8-month professional certificate course in SAP consulting on Coursera. This gave me a solid foundation in SAP, including understanding its structure and the process of solution implementation.

Now I'm looking for ways to gain real-world, practical experience. I'd love to hear any suggestions you have on how to bridge the gap between my training and real-world application.

entry.title: Re: New Depreciation Area: Where are T093-VIEW0/1/2/3/4/5/6 configured?
entry.content: Hi Can any one please assist, Where are those views configured in the table of T093 fields VIEW0/1/2/3/4/5.?

entry.title: How to enable caching for SAP OData V2?
entry.content:
Hello SAP experts!

May I enquire on how can I register my OData service for caching?

I referred to these guides by SAP and I was able to add my service in transaction code /UI2/CACHE.

After that, I'm kind of lost by SAP's guides:

Cache for OData Services | SAP Help Portal

Registering an OData Service for Caching | SAP Help Portal

My situation:
I tried creating an OData V2 for table MARA to discover its capabilities using the service implementation below. I noticed that every time I do client-paging ($top and $skip), data repeatedly gets queried from HANA database for every request/page which makes it inefficient.

My plan:

I thought maybe there's a way to (1) cache the queried data and do the client-paging from there. And (2) after sending to the requestor all pages of data, cache can now be emptied.

Steps I've done so far:

1) implement the OData for MARA with full and delta capabilities, dynamic select and dynamic where clauses.

 

 

METHOD mara_get_entityset.

* =================================================================================================
*   Description :     Fetch MARA data based on filter and selection criteria.
*   Author      :     
*   Date        :     
*
*   Parameters:
*      it_filter_select_options     : Table containing filter criteria
*      iv_filter_string             : Dynamic filter string for SQL WHERE clause
*      io_tech_request_context      : Context providing technical request details
*      et_entityset                 : Output table containing the result set
*      es_response_context          : Structure for response metadata
*
*   Purpose:
*      This method extracts data from the MARA table based on provided
*      filters from the OData URL. It handles both full and delta loads,
*      processes $top and $skip parameters for pagination, and supports
*      dynamic field selection through $select queries.
* =================================================================================================


* =================================================================================================
*   Prepare variables
* =================================================================================================
  "Declare
  DATA:
    lv_select                     TYPE string,
    lt_mara                       TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF mara.

  "Initialize
  CLEAR:
    lv_select,
    lt_mara[],
    et_entityset[].


* =================================================================================================
*   Retrieve $filter values
* =================================================================================================
  "Scan all filters from request
  LOOP AT it_filter_select_options ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_f_so>).

    "Check if LOAD filter is present
    IF <lfs_f_so>-property = 'LOAD'.

      "Read the first selection only of LOAD filter
      READ TABLE <lfs_f_so>-select_options ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_so>) INDEX 1.

        "Check if LOAD filter is 'DELTA'
        IF to_upper( <lfs_so>-low ) = 'DELTA'.
          DATA(lv_load) = to_upper( <lfs_so>-low ).
          EXIT.
        ENDIF.

    ENDIF.

  ENDLOOP.


* =================================================================================================
*   Retrieve $select values
* =================================================================================================
  "Scan all selected fields from request
  DATA(lt_select) = io_tech_request_context->get_select( ).

  "Build dynamic select string
  LOOP AT lt_select ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_select>).

    IF <lfs_select> IS INITIAL.
      lv_select = <lfs_select>.
    ELSE.
      lv_select = |{ lv_select }, { <lfs_select> }|.
    ENDIF.

  ENDLOOP.

  "Set dynamic select to select ALL
  IF lv_select IS INITIAL.
    lv_select = '*'.
  ENDIF.


* =================================================================================================
*   Query data from database
* =================================================================================================
  "Execute delta load
  IF lv_load = 'DELTA'.

    DATA(lv_yesterday) = sy-datum - 1.

    "Extract data from yesterday to future
    SELECT (lv_select)
      FROM mara
      INTO TABLE lt_mara
      WHERE ( ersda >= lv_yesterday AND laeda = '00000000' )
        OR ( laeda >= lv_yesterday AND laeda <> '00000000' )
      ORDER BY matnr ASCENDING.

  "Execute full load
  ELSE.

    IF iv_filter_string IS NOT INITIAL.

      "Query data using $filter
      SELECT (lv_select)
        FROM mara
        INTO TABLE lt_mara
        WHERE (iv_filter_string)
        ORDER BY matnr ASCENDING.

    ELSE.

      "Query all data
      SELECT (lv_select)
        FROM mara
        INTO TABLE lt_mara
        ORDER BY matnr ASCENDING.

    ENDIF.

  ENDIF.

  "Get row count
  DATA(lv_count) = sy-dbcnt.


* =================================================================================================
*   Prepare data for integration
* =================================================================================================
  "Check if the query returned data
  IF lt_mara IS NOT INITIAL.

    "Check if request has $count
    IF io_tech_request_context->has_count( ) = abap_true.

      "Respond with row count of dataset
      es_response_context-count = lv_count.

    ELSE.

      "Retrieve $top and $skip query options
      DATA(lv_top) = io_tech_request_context->get_top( ).
      DATA(lv_skip) = io_tech_request_context->get_skip( ).

      "Check if paging is included in request
      IF lv_top IS NOT INITIAL OR lv_skip IS NOT INITIAL.

        DATA(lv_end_index) = lv_skip + lv_top.

        "Set ending index to row count to prevent out-of-bounds issue
        IF lv_end_index > lv_count.
          lv_end_index = lv_count.
        ENDIF.

        "Respond with paginated dataset
        LOOP AT lt_mara FROM lv_skip + 1 TO lv_end_index ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_mara>).
          APPEND CORRESPONDING #( <ls_mara> ) TO et_entityset.
        ENDLOOP.

        "Release memory occupied
        FREE:
          lv_top,
          lv_skip,
          lv_end_index.

      ELSE.

        "Respond with whole dataset
        et_entityset = CORRESPONDING #( lt_mara ).

      ENDIF.

    ENDIF.

    "Release memory occupied
    FREE:
      lv_load,
      lv_select,
      lv_count,
      lt_mara[].

  ENDIF.

ENDMETHOD.

 

2) add the service of MARA OData to /UI2/CACHE

Couple notes:
1) Im using the OData to send data to Azure/Databricks Lakehouse

2) We are only using SAP ECC on HANA with runtime license so my options with integration are limited.

 

Hope you can help me with cache-ing thank you!

entry.title: Re: Production Order Settlement with TECO/DLV Status
entry.content: thank you.

entry.title: Exporting and Importing Custom Objects and CPE Packages in SAP Sustainability Control Tower
entry.content:
This blog provides a broad outline of how custom objects like custom dimensions, custom measures and custom metrics can be exported and imported between different tenants, as well as the exporting and importing of CPE packages.

Restriction: Currently, we have no option to export and import the following objects.

  • KPI Tiles
  • Ambitions
  • Ambitions Groups
  • Leading and supported reporting hierarchy

Fact Sheet: Once we have defined our own set of custom dimensions, custom measures and custom metrics, we can export that set using either of the respective apps, i.e. Manage Custom Dimensions, Manage Measures, Manage Metrics. We can also export and import the created CPE (Calculation and Processing Engine) content.

To begin with, let us focus on how we can export and import custom objects. For Instance I have created a custom dimension called "Insurance Type For Employees" and updated the master data. 

Exporting and Importing Custom Objects:

Now let us choose "Go To Export" option.

Add the desired custom objects.

Select the desired custom dimension from the value help and add to the custom objects inventory.

All valid custom objects can be exported using the option "Export All".

we can find the exported file in the "Downloads" folder with the file name as "sct-custom-inventory.json" and can be renamed if required for import.

Now, let us access "Manage Custom Dimensions" app to import.

choose the file to upload,

Let us choose the option "Import All". System imports the valid custom objects.

 

Exporting and Importing CPE Packages:

Let us look at how we can export and import content from CPE packages. We can enter CPE from various possible ways. For instance I have chosen using app "CPE Process Templates for Data Management".

Let us move on to environments overview page,

from the overview page, we can select the desired package to export by choosing the option "Export".

For instance, I have chosen "SCT - Manage ESG Data", " Version 0009".

A file in the .zip file format is added to the list with the name and version of the environment. We can view the exported environment.

 

 

Now, let us import using file upload option.

After the file upload has been successful, we must choose the option "Import" and input the details.

Let us choose "Import" and check the import status.

 

 😊This brings me to the end of the "Exporting and Importing Custom Objects" walkthrough process in SAP Sustainability Control Tower. I hope this blog proves useful to the Sustainability Control Tower Team. I think this feature brings a significant value to the business during implementation process.

If you want to gain a deep understanding of  SAP Sustainability Control Tower and out of the box seamless integration with SAP EHSM, Environment Management, check out my SAP SCT & EHSM blogs mentioned below, they will give you a broad outline of SAP Sustainability Control Tower  & SAP EHSM Environment Management topics.

  1. European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS) 
  2. EHS - Environment Management, Emission Management Part-1 
  3. EHS - Environment Management, Emission Management Part-2 
  4. EHS - Environment Management, Emission Management Part-3 
  5. SAP Sustainability Control Tower-Import Greenhouse Gas Emissions from SAP EHS Environment Management 
  6. Addendum to SAP Sustainability Control Tower-Import Greenhouse Gas Emissions from SAP EHSM-EM 
  7. SAP Sustainability Control Tower(SCT) Metric Management-Basic Custom Metric & Standard Custom Metric 

--

Yours Sincerely,

Pardhasaradhi Reddy.C

entry.title: How to kill user session in sap bo rest api?
entry.content:
Hi,

I am using sap bo rest apis in my C# application.

The user get token and requests the relevant services to access the data, but a user can have more than one session or a session can be open for a long time, which causes the system to crash. How can I kill these sessions for a certain period of time with Sap Bo Rest API?

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP, Kyma runtime: APIRule migration - timelines
entry.content:
hello @FabioVarisco!
that is really good question 🙂 v1* API was heavily based on ORY Oathkeeper API, while v2* API went into direction of Istio API that is exclusively used under the hood of APIRule controller. While we already did architectural change we took opportunity to change APIRule API. Our "side goal" was to simplify and flatten APIRule API. That is the reason why you will not find `accessStrategies` in v2 API. For more details check out APIRule documentation.

Cheers, 
Magda

entry.title: Unable to Find Due Date Field in "Manage Billing Documents" App
entry.content:
I am using the "Manage Billing Documents" app in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and need to check the Due Date for billing documents.

I cannot find a field displaying the Due Date in this app.

Could you please confirm whether the Due Date field is available in this app? If not, what is the recommended approach to view the Due Date for a billing document directly?

Additionally, if the field is not available, is there a way to enable it via Adapt UI or any other configuration?

Thank you for your support.

entry.title: Re: Gts commodity code transfer to S4
entry.content: Hi Dean, yes these are activated as per OSS 235566. But I am unsure about the third step "Use transaction BD52 to maintain the following entries for the logical message type /SAPSLL/CLSMD_SLL" as this does not seem possible because object /SAPSLL/CTSGEN does not exist.

entry.title: Issue with mbt build in Newly Created CAP Project on SAP BTP Trial
entry.content:
I have created a CAP Project in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) using the following steps:

  1. File → New Project From Template → CAP Project
  2. Provided:
    • Project Name: sample
    • Runtime: Node.js
    • Database: PostgreSQL
    • Deployment Type: Cloud Foundry (MTA Deployment)
    • Runtime Capability: SAP BTP Authorization and Trust Management Service
    • Sample Content: Minimal Sample

The project was successfully created and was functioning as expected when running locally.

However, when attempting to execute mbt build to package the project for deployment, the following error occurs:
[2025-02-05 06:23:23] INFO validating the MTA project
[2025-02-05 06:23:23] ERROR the "mta.yaml" file is not valid:
line 33: the "gen/pg" path of the "sample-postgres-deployer" module does not exist
make: *** [Makefile_20250205062318.mta:46: validate] Error 1
Error: could not build the MTA project: could not execute the "make -f Makefile_20250205062318.mta p=cf mtar= strict=true mode=" command: exit status 2.


Notably, mbt build works fine for my other existing projects. The issue seems to be specific to this newly created project.
I would appreciate any insights into the root cause of this issue and any recommended resolution steps.
Thank you for your support.

entry.title: Re: Meet the SAP BTP Services for Spend: SAP Build
entry.content: Nice initiative! Best Wishes 👍

entry.title: Re: S4 HANA Public Cloud Extensibility
Re: Contract Management - Delta Accruals for a fixed amou...
entry.content: Thanks for this Gauthier will test the approach and let you know how we get on. These contracts have some conditions that may be maintained as fixed amounts others that might be a percentage so removing the business volume from the contract type is not really an option but utilising the settlement material may be. Will let you know how it goes!

entry.title: Re: S4 HANA Public Cloud Extensibility
entry.content:
Hello @tajshaik3 

Communication user which is created in Maintain Communication Users app, has to have same password with user which is created in Users for Outbound Communication section of Communication System app.

Also please check if both users are locked or not.

entry.title: Re: Rental server for SAP S/4HANA Cloud version up
entry.content:
Hi,

Yes this is possible if you are already subscribing to RISE with SAP.

The sandboxes is what we call additional tiers and are available in sizes corresponding to the sizes you find for RISE with SAP. The additional tiers are sold for a minimum of 3 months.

Best Regards
Henrik Strange

entry.title: Re: Facing Disconnection from SAP application to DB for every 20 minutes
entry.content: Is a virus scanner running on the affected server?

entry.title: Position Vacancy Flag in Crossboarding
entry.content:
Hi SF experts,

Is there any documentation or can anyone confirm that the vacancy flag in Position is not being updated when using Crossboarding?

entry.title: Re: How to configure IP based access to SAP Sales and Service Version 2
entry.content:
Thank you for the details and good informations on setting up the SSO and IP based connection restriction! @SunkariPraveen 

Please let me know about useful informations about Sales Cloud Version 2 like this!!

You are a TRUE HERO!

Sincerely,

JG_LEE

entry.title: Re: What is TAANA in SAP?
entry.content: In Which SAP system TAANA is used for maintaining entries? As for as I know TAANA is used for table analysis. It doesn't allow creation/modification of table data.

entry.title: Re: sap build code authorization error: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading '$count')
entry.content: Hi @mostafa4, regarding your question, I have tried to reproduce the same steps as yours. Have you added the corresponding role into your BTP user? I did it, and re-login the BTP subaccount and verified it after waiting for a few moments, the `Sales` column data is hidden as expected. Would you double check it?

entry.title: Re: Extract Signal Queries into Document or PDF in SAP Si...
entry.content:
Hello, this is not something that is supported directly within the product no.

It is possible to "export" a dashboard as a JSON file though, and then find the queries used inside the dashboard directly in the json file. This could be done manually with a text editor, or automated with a short python script for example that extracts the query info from the json file.

Best, Sam.

entry.title: Re: SKU 8011336 - Add-on required for SAP Spatial Asset M...
entry.content:
Hi,

SAP Spatial Asset Management for S/4HANA private cloud edition will require 
SAP Geographical Enablement Framework 1.0 SPS14 (SAP GEF 1.0) (Technical name ‘UIGEF001’) to be installed and HANA Content ‘HCO_GEF_DATA 100'.

SAP Note 2383937 describe how to install the Geographical Enablement Framework.

Best Regards
Henrik Strange
"

entry.title: HANA XSA Fiori-Elements
entry.content:
Hi,

we would like to implement Fiori Elements apps in the WebIDE (SAP HANA 2.0 SPS 08 XSA). Currently, only the Fiori List Report template is available, and it requires a v2 OData service as a basis, which is surprising since Fiori Elements was actually designed for OData v4. Will the other templates be integrated in future releases, and will they support the v4 version accordingly?

Br,

Michael

entry.title: Re: Reconciliation acct : Is a required entry field
entry.content:
Hi,

"Reconciliation acct : Is a required entry field" error message can appear when you fill the AKONT field in the current company code screen, but in the BP master data you have one or more company codes that haven't been maintained. Please check if there are any company codes that haven't been maintained when you click on Company Codes button.

entry.title: Re: sap build code authorization error: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading '$count')
entry.content: hi @xinye sorry for the confusion the problem in the second code is that any additional queries will not work as I return the same book data. for example in the ui if i use the search field to search for a title or a author it will always return all the data and the same for the filters. I asked joule to generate code to help me but it code always gave me the error Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading '$count').

entry.title: Assign multiple valuation methodes in FCV to one Valiualtion rule
entry.content:
Hello,

I want to use different valuation methods for various balance sheet items, identified by different Sem. Tags, within the same valuation run.

Specifically, I want to value all items with the SemTag "FX" using the "always valuate" method, and items with the SemTag "FX2" using the lower of cost or market principle.

 

SAP is giving me an error that you can't assign multiple valuation methods to one valuation rule. However, I can only link one valuation rule to my accounting principle.

 

How can I value different items differently in SAP?

Business Background:

Current assets should always be valued.

Loans, however, should be valued according to the lower of cost or market principle.

Alternative Solution:

Do you know a workaround?

I'm thinking of something like mirroring the foreign currency g/l job and running the loan valuations as a separate job. Is this possible?

Let me know if you need further assistance or clarification.

entry.title: Re: How to restrict authorization for contract activation...
entry.content: Hi Reddy! Thanks for replying. Which of the authorization objects should I update so I can restrict the activation? I checked but I can't seem to find the authorization object for the activation. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: S4 PCE sandbox for pre conversion system
entry.content:
Hi Patrick,

If the target state of the sandbox is S/4 then you should use the S/4 Additional tier as indicated in your table.

The initial installation of the sandbox can be ordered as a copy of the ERP production system. Cost of this is included. However you you plan to do this multiple times for the same sandbox, the additional attempts will require additional cost.

Best Regards
Henrik Strange

entry.title: SAP Community Call for ALM – APJ edition
entry.content:
The monthly webinar - focusing on the APJ region and timezone - provides everything you need to know about SAP's Application Lifecycle Management (ALM) offerings. Get the latest news and regional updates directly from SAP's topic experts!

By attending this call you have the chance to stay updated about what's going on with SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Focused Run.

For slides and recordings click here. 

Link to register is coming soon!

entry.title: Re: What is shown under 'Latest replies'?
entry.content:
Hi @AnnePetteroe,

A great improvement!

In addition, can we get a "Sort Ascending / Descending" option and a "show x posts" option, preferably with a maximum of at least 100?

For example, when looking at the list filtered by "Q&A No Accepted Solution", and sorted by "Creation Date", it would be great if we could sort that from old to new as well, so that older unanswered questions are not drowned out by newer posts.

Another example, when sorted by "Most views", we now always get the most views on top. This is not very usefull, because those questions are more likely to already have valid answers. Questions with little or no views are much more interesting. For other type content it will circumvent "click-bait" posts, and really old posts, that will have naturally gathered more views, drowning out newer ones that have not yet gotten a lot of views.

And if we want to push the envelope, sorting by multiple criteria...?

entry.title: Hands On outdated: Developing and Extending SAP Fiori Elements Apps
entry.content:
Hi!
I am doing the course "Developing and Extending SAP Fiori Elements Apps" and have a problem with the hands on exercises. The course is from 2021 and in between the user interface, e.g. the guided development tool, changed a lot.
Is it possible to set up my Application Studio to an older version?

Sorry, I can't select any other area to post this question. It belongs to SAP Learning.

Regards, Ralf

entry.title: Re: IAS for External Learner and Onboardee
entry.content: My customer has the same issue for Pre-Day 1. We used the Employee ID from EC/Platform to populate the User ID field in LMS. But after the users are created in LMS via connector, they cannot access Learning with error "Failed to authenticate SAML response...". Initially, I mapped the User ID from EC/Platform

entry.title: SAP CodeJam: Connecting systems and services using SAP Integration Suite (Dubai, UAE)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam instance will be about SAP Integration Suite and how we can use it to connect systems and services in our landscape. Join us for this 5-hour event of developer networking and hands-on learning in Dubai, UAE.

We will achieve an integration scenario like the one below in the diagram by the end of the CodeJam.

 

If you are interested in what we will develop during the event, check out the Connecting Systems and Services using SAP Integration Suite CodeJam GitHub repo -  https://github.com/SAP-samples/connecting-systems-services-integration-suite-codejam. The repository contains the details of the integration scenario we will use during the event.

This is a free hands-on, in-person event only (not virtual) and takes place Tuesday, the 25th of March, in Dubai, UAE @ the Emirates Financial Towers (2308 23rd floor, South Tower, Emirates Financial Tower, DIFC). The event is kindly being hosted by Mark9 solutions, thank you! 

The instructor is Developer Advocate Antonio Maradiaga.

Every attendee must complete essential prerequisites before arriving at the event location. You can find the prerequisites here: https://github.com/SAP-samples/connecting-systems-services-integration-suite-codejam/blob/main/prerequisites.md.

If you wish to attend, please indicate that using the "I'm attending" facility on this page. Please note that spaces are limited - we can only accommodate 30 attendees for this event. In that context, if you indicate that you're attending and later realise you can't, please remove your name so that others get a chance instead. Thanks!

Location: 2308 23rd floor, South Tower, Emirates Financial Tower, DIFC

entry.title: Re: The re-use of current Contents server which connect t...
entry.content:
Hi Chang,

The strategic direction from SAP is to use BTP Document Managment Services.

For an on-prem customer, the license for Content Server is part of the SAP ERP/Netweaver license.
Customer can re-use his on-prem content servers for his S/4 RISE system if he retain his on-prem license that allows the use of Content Server.

Customer can also purchase Content Server in PCE. The cost of this covers the infrastructure and operational cost needed to run the Content Servers in cloud.

Best Regards
Henrik Strange

entry.title: Re: Unable to Find Due Date Field in "Manage Billing Docu...
entry.content:
Hello,

The "Manage Billing Documents" app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud is primarily intended for viewing and managing billing documents. As of the information I have, the app does not explicitly mention a Due Date field being available directly within its standard interface.

If the Due Date field is not visible in the app, here are some recommendations:

Adapt UI: SAP's Adapt UI feature may allow you to customize the app to display additional fields if they are available in the metadata. If Due Date is part of the metadata that can be exposed through UI adaptation, you could possibly add it to your visible fields in the app. To explore this, your user role must have authorization to adapt UI layouts.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: How to Change Reconciliation Account in MIRO,MIR7,MIRA,MIR4 with using BADI’s
entry.content:
@Filipe_Magalhaes 

Thanks for sharing the OSS note, However it seems that it's only applicable for Russia as it is a Localization for Russia only. below is the quote.

"exist in Business function FIN_LOC_CI_14, EhP6 as enhanced functionality of Financial Accounting Localization for Russia only.

Did you still add the functionality to your non-Russian SAP environment?

entry.title: Re: SAP Rise IaaS have backup or not ?
entry.content: Yes, everything on top of OS is managed by customer

entry.title: Re: Initial Setup – Preparing for SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Signavio Integration
entry.content: Great, thanks for sharing sir 😊

entry.title: Change Offlinestore without sending a request
entry.content:
I am wondering if there is a possibility to make changes to an entity in offline store without creating an entry in the RequestQueue and send a Request to the backend with the next Upload.

The background is that I have one Online and one Offline service. When the user is making changes in Offline mode all changes are made in the offline store. With the next Upload they are uploaded as usual.

But when the user is making changes in Online mode I am making the change in both Online and Offline service. Online to send the requests Immediately and get the responses from backend, so that the user can react directly to the response from backend.
Offline because I want to have my offline store in sync and want to be able to show the changes to the user in the frontend.
But this means that I make changes to Offline Store that are now in my RequestQueue but they were already sent to the backend via OnlineService.
So what I'm looking for is a way to make changes to the offline storage that won't be uploaded and will be overwritten the next time I synchronize. Is this possible?

I am using MDK Client 24.7 iOS

entry.title: Credit exposure incorrect data
entry.content:
Credit Exposure is not updated correctly for the customer.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Credit exposure incorrect data
entry.content: Please use the job template "Rebuild Credit Exposure" help portal link

entry.title: Re: SAP Rise IaaS have backup or not ?
entry.content: how about the OS level backup, snapshot, etc? if OS level corrupt after windows update, any backup from SAP? and if the server is hang, customer can call SAP PCE to restart ?

entry.title: Re: Copy ACTUALSQTYPRIORYEAR values are shifted by 10 weeks
entry.content:
Roberto,

You are on one of the touchy fields in IBP. 62, 52, 12, 53, etc when it comes to timeshift.

You have 2 options for perform timeshift

1- Using IBP_PERIODSHIFT allows shifting in the number of periods of the base level of the keyf itself. In your case techweek hence no chance as  10 techweeks are often < 10 weeks. Either you decide for an empirical 11 tech weeks check that in average in outlook you could figure out 10 calweeks relate most of the time to 11 techweek. THis is my preferred as this is supported with a very simple formula

2- You set an attribute transformation (ATTTRF), which I am opposed because of maintenance and complexity, to shift the value. THere in SAPIBP1 former version it was done like you explain with 62 on techweek plevel, as in average 1 year = 62 techweek. Not any better to option 1 in fact and much more complex. However may be with ATTTRF yu could try the transfo on the CALWEEK level, maybe this works but I have serious doubts and personally I would not invest 10 minutes even

 

Then Option 3, that is not realtime unfortunately. Use Copy operator to shift by 10 calweek. Not sure again you can define CALWEEK whereas the keyf is TECHWEEK

Then Option 4. Discussing with SAP development last year, they will soon propose an alternative for ATTRTRF based on a formula I suggested instead of the current complexity. Here maybe there would be some goodies. Don't know yet.

Cheers, Daniel

entry.title: Re: sap build code authorization error: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading '$count')
entry.content: Hi @mostafa4, thanks for the clarification, I got your point now. Per my testing, it looks your requirement can not be achieved from the custom logic. Have you tried the alternative option? For example, create a new service projection entity without `Sales`, and assign role to this new service entity only?

entry.title: Re: Does App server Scale-up from 64 GB to 128 GB change...
entry.content:
Hi,

Yes when upgrading the server memory the server instance will change to a new one with the desired amount of memory like Virtual-128GiB-m6a.8xlarge.

But we see regular updates to what the hyperscalers makes available so the actual server instance could differ and depend on the actual availability in the data center used.

Best Regards
Henrik Strange

entry.title: Re: SAP Material Master
entry.content:
For me, who only knows MM IM, it was very helpful to understand MM Retail a little more.
Thanl you.

entry.title: Re: ABAP to XML and vice versa
entry.content: Hi Tudor, I really appreciate the blog. 11 years later it is still the best I can find if it concernes dynamic XML handling.

entry.title: Re: Cannot import a PD LM into ER/Studio using the MetaIn...
entry.content:
Hi George,

I sometimes run into similar issues, and quite often, the reason turns out to be an "unsupported" character in one of the Text attributes (both standard and extended). So I'd start by wiping Descriptions, Annotations, and Comments from all major LDM objects (Entity, Entity Attribute, Domain, Rule, and perhaps Identifiers if necessary…). If the import succeeds after that, you'll know where to dig.

And if not, it was still worth a shot—wiping these takes just four minutes, whether you script it or do it manually in batch using lists in the Model menu. (I know you know this—just mentioning it for others. 😊)

Running the internal check might also help.

Have a nice day,

Ondrej Divis

entry.title: Re: Striking a Balance: Navigating the Open-Source Tightrope in a World of Evolving Threats - Part 1
entry.content:
Hi Philip,

Thanks a lot for the article. It was well written!
I am looking forward to Part 2, and also insights into how SAP is balancing OSS Risks and Benefits.

Kind Regards
Viet

entry.title: Is it possible that the downpayment on the FA is reversed at the time of posting GR?
entry.content:
Hi,

In the ECAR PO process including a down payment, the down payment is reversed automatically when the final payment is posted. Until the final payment is processed, the value of the asset is overestimated by the down payment value.

Is it possible that the downpayment on the FA is reversed at the time of posting the PO goods receipt?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Variant update problem with variant management contro...
entry.content:
Hi Bilen,

I was able to resolve the issue by ensuring that the variant data is persistently stored rather than being directly assigned to filterData. The key improvement was persisting variants in localStorage, merging new data with existing data before saving.

 

variantManagerXML.attachEvent("save", (event) => {
    try {
        const filterData = mainFilter.getData();
        const mParameters = event.getParameters();
        let currentVariantKey = mParameters.key || this.byId("vm").getCurrentVariantKey();
        if (!currentVariantKey) return;

        // Retrieve existing custom variants from localStorage
        const customVariantData = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("customVariantData")) || {};
        const existingVariant = customVariantData[currentVariantKey] || {};

        // Merge new filter values with existing variant data
        customVariantData[currentVariantKey] = {
            ...existingVariant,
            XYZ: { values: [...filterData.XYZ.values] }
        };

        // Persist the updated variant only if overwriting is enabled
        if (mParameters.overwrite) {
            localStorage.setItem("customVariantData", JSON.stringify(customVariantData));
        }

        const sanitizedData = JSON.parse(
            JSON.stringify(filterData, (key, value) => 
                key === "values" && Array.isArray(value) ? JSON.stringify(value) : value
            )
        );
        mParameters.contexts = {
            content: JSON.stringify(sanitizedData)
        };

        mParameters.executeOnSelection = true;
    } catch (error) {
        console.error("Error during save:", error);
    }
});

 

I hope this helps anyone else looking for a similar solution!
Best,
Nil

entry.title: Re: RISE Base Option VPN amount
entry.content:
Hi,

Yes there is by default up to 5 S2S VPN connections added to SAP RISE Base option.

More can be purchased with the Connection Packages.

Henrik Strange

entry.title: SAP Master Data Integration with Field Service Management
entry.content:
Hello SAP Gurus,

i have a question regarding SAP Master Data Integration with Field Service Management and S4/HANA Cloud.

It is stated, that for each connecting system you need a service instance for Master Data Integration in SAP BTP.

According to this link:

https://help.sap.com/docs/master-data-integration/sap-master-data-integration-prod/connecting-applications-via-service-instances

I Should create a instance for SAP Field Service Management aswell.

But what should i choose for the mandatory instance parameter "application" ?

FSM is not listed at all.

Thanks in Advance.

Regards

Simon

entry.title: Re: How to check your custom code for ABAP Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @sivani,

yes, ATC checks only custom code (not SAP standard code) in the standard namespaces Y* and Z*. If you use another custom namespace, you need to register it to be checked with ATC, see please Registering Name Spaces.

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: Naming Company Code and Trading Partner
entry.content:
Hello, 
after consulting with experts, I'm able to offer an answer: 

From an Accounting and Group Reporting perspective, there is no issue in using the same naming for company code and company/trading partner. It is actually very common to have the same naming for company code and company in ICMR.
Note: ICMR mainly runs on company or consolidation unit, though.

Kind regards,
Mareike

entry.title: SAP Community Call for ALM – APJ edition
entry.content:
The monthly webinar - focusing on the APJ region and timezone - provides everything you need to know about SAP's Application Lifecycle Management (ALM) offerings. Get the latest news and regional updates directly from SAP's topic experts!

By attending this call you have the chance to stay updated about what's going on with SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Focused Run.

For slides and recordings click here. 

Link to register is coming soon!

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori Launchpad: New Fast and Easy Option to Adapt SAP-delivered Launchpad Content (for On
entry.content:
Hi Manasa,

> What are the possible issues in earlier releases (SAP Front-end server for SAP S/4HANA 2021)
> if we adapt the SAP delivered content.

in earlier releases (e.g. SAP Front-end server for SAP S/4HANA 2021) the adaptation feature is not available.

So, to reach the goal of adaptation, you have to copy the launchpad app descriptor item into a custom technical catalog and adjust all business catalogs, pages, spaces, groups and roles containing this app - you have to make sure that all these entities point to your copied app and not to the original.

If you still use the tool Launchpad Designer, you can also copy the tiles and target mappings and adjust all business catalogs, pages, spaces, groups and roles containing them.  

These methods do not have issues, but they are more work and you have to make sure you do not overlook any references that point to the originals instead of to your copies.

So, in earlier releases, if you can use the SAP-delivered content as is, that's easiest.

Best regards,
Ruth

entry.title: Re: Posting Delivery via DTW
entry.content:
Hi @Jeerick ,

I can only reply by taking the error message into account, which says the serial/batch you're using doesn't exist. 

Certain SAP Business One document types (e.g., Goods Receipt, Inventory Transfers) require serial or batch numbers to exist in the system before being referenced. This is especially true for processes like issuing stock or transferring inventory where the serial/batch data is pre-defined during receipt or creation of stock.

Before importing a document, verify that the serial or batch numbers you are referencing have been created in SAP Business One. These are typically added during stock transactions like Goods Receipt or Purchase Invoice.

And last but not least: Have you tried to post a delivery with the same batch details from the SBO client instead? Depending on the result, this could give us an idea of where the issue is.

I hope it helps.

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Re: Translate values ​​of a field in different languages...
entry.content:
Hi,

To translate the values of a list type field so that they appear in different languages, you can use the Value Translations feature. This allows the field values to dynamically change based on the user's logged-in language.

Please check the following page in our documentation about Value Translations for more information:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIELD_SERVICE_MANAGEMENT/fsm_admin/value-translations.html

On this page, you'll find a table listing the supported object types, which will help you understand how to implement value translations for your specific use case.

entry.title: Industry 5.0: The Next Revolution in Manufacturing
entry.content:
Introduction

The industrial landscape is undergoing a seismic shift. As we transition from the hyper-automated, data-driven era of Industry 4.0 to the human-centric, sustainable, and resilient framework of Industry 5.0, businesses face both unprecedented challenges and opportunities. At the heart of this evolution lies the integration of advanced technologies like AI, IoT, and collaborative robots (cobots) with human creativity and ethical practices. In this blog, we explore how SAP, a global leader in enterprise software, is uniquely positioned to drive this transformation, enabling organizations to thrive in the Industry 5.0 era.

Understanding Industry 5.0

Industry 5.0 represents a shift from the automation-centric approach of Industry 4.0 to a more human-centric model. It focuses on leveraging the strengths of both humans and machines to create a symbiotic relationship. This collaboration enables more personalized and flexible production processes, catering to the unique needs and preferences of individual customers.

Key characteristics of Industry 5.0 include:

  1. Human-Machine Collaboration: Unlike Industry 4.0, which primarily focused on automation, Industry 5.0 emphasizes the collaboration between humans and machines. This synergy allows for more creative and innovative solutions.

  2. Personalization: Industry 5.0 enables mass customization, where products are tailored to individual customer preferences. This personalization is achieved through the integration of advanced technologies and human insights.

  3. Sustainability: With a growing emphasis on environmental responsibility, Industry 5.0 aims to create more sustainable manufacturing processes. This includes reducing waste, conserving resources, and minimizing the environmental impact of production.

  4. Resilience: The COVID-19 pandemic highlighted the need for resilient supply chains. Industry 5.0 focuses on building robust and adaptable manufacturing systems that can withstand disruptions and quickly recover from unforeseen events.

The Evolution of Industrial Revolutions

To fully appreciate the significance of Industry 5.0, it's essential to understand the evolution of industrial revolutions. The first industrial revolution, which began in the late 18th century, was characterized by the mechanization of production using water and steam power. This period saw the introduction of machines that replaced manual labor, significantly increasing productivity.

The second industrial revolution, in the late 19th and early 20th centuries, brought about mass production and assembly lines. This era was marked by the widespread use of electricity and the development of new manufacturing processes, leading to the rise of large-scale industries.

The third industrial revolution, starting in the late 20th century, introduced digital technologies and automation. This period saw the adoption of computers, robotics, and information technology, transforming manufacturing processes and enabling greater efficiency and precision.

Industry 4.0, which emerged in the early 21st century, focused on the integration of digital technologies and the Internet of Things (IoT). This era emphasized automation, data exchange, and the use of cyber-physical systems to create smart factories. Industry 4.0 technologies, such as artificial intelligence, machine learning, and big data analytics, have revolutionized manufacturing processes, enabling real-time monitoring, predictive maintenance, and enhanced operational efficiency.

The Shift to Industry 5.0

While Industry 4.0 has brought about significant advancements, it has also highlighted the limitations of a purely automation-centric approach. The focus on automation and digital technologies often overlooks the unique strengths and creativity of human workers. Industry 5.0 aims to address this gap by fostering a more collaborative relationship between humans and machines.

In Industry 5.0, humans and machines work together, leveraging their respective strengths to create innovative solutions. This collaboration enables more personalized and flexible production processes, catering to the unique needs and preferences of individual customers. By integrating human insights with advanced technologies, Industry 5.0 aims to create a more sustainable and resilient manufacturing landscape.

The Role of SAP in Industry 5.0

SAP, a global leader in enterprise software, is poised to play a pivotal role in the transition to Industry 5.0. With its comprehensive suite of solutions, SAP can help businesses navigate the complexities of this new industrial era and unlock unprecedented value.

  1. Intelligent Technologies: SAP's intelligent technologies, such as machine learning, artificial intelligence, and the Internet of Things (IoT), enable real-time data analysis and predictive insights. These technologies facilitate human-machine collaboration, allowing for more informed decision-making and enhanced operational efficiency.

  2. Customer Experience: SAP's Customer Experience (CX) solutions help businesses understand and anticipate customer needs. By leveraging data analytics and personalization tools, companies can deliver tailored products and services, enhancing customer satisfaction and loyalty.

  3. Sustainable Operations: SAP's sustainability solutions support businesses in achieving their environmental goals. From carbon footprint tracking to waste management, SAP provides the tools needed to create more sustainable manufacturing processes.

  4. Supply Chain Resilience: SAP's supply chain management solutions help businesses build resilient and adaptable supply chains. By integrating real-time data and predictive analytics, companies can quickly respond to disruptions and ensure business continuity.

  5. Human-Centric Design: SAP's human-centric design principles focus on creating user-friendly and intuitive solutions. By prioritizing the user experience, SAP enables seamless collaboration between humans and machines, fostering innovation and creativity.

Personalizing the Manufacturing Experience

One of the most exciting aspects of Industry 5.0 is the ability to personalize the manufacturing experience. In today's fast-paced world, customers demand products that are tailored to their unique needs and preferences. Industry 5.0 enables mass customization, where products are designed and manufactured to meet individual customer requirements.

SAP's solutions play a crucial role in enabling this personalization. By leveraging data analytics and customer insights, businesses can gain a deeper understanding of customer preferences and behaviors. This information can be used to create personalized products and services, enhancing customer satisfaction and loyalty.

For example, a clothing manufacturer can use SAP's solutions to analyze customer data and identify trends and preferences. This information can be used to design and produce customized clothing items that meet the unique needs of individual customers. By integrating human insights with advanced technologies, the manufacturer can create a more personalized and flexible production process, catering to the diverse needs of its customer base.

Enhancing Sustainability in Manufacturing

Sustainability is a critical aspect of Industry 5.0. With a growing emphasis on environmental responsibility, businesses are increasingly focused on creating more sustainable manufacturing processes. SAP's solutions support businesses in achieving their sustainability goals, from reducing waste and conserving resources to minimizing the environmental impact of production.

For instance, a food manufacturer can use SAP's sustainability solutions to track and manage its carbon footprint. By analyzing data on energy consumption, waste generation, and resource usage, the manufacturer can identify areas for improvement and implement sustainable practices. This not only helps in reducing the environmental impact of production but also enhances the manufacturer's reputation as a responsible and sustainable business.

Building Resilient Supply Chains

The COVID-19 pandemic has highlighted the importance of resilient supply chains. Industry 5.0 focuses on building robust and adaptable manufacturing systems that can withstand disruptions and quickly recover from unforeseen events. SAP's supply chain management solutions play a crucial role in enabling this resilience.

By integrating real-time data and predictive analytics, businesses can gain visibility into their supply chains and identify potential disruptions. This information can be used to develop contingency plans and ensure business continuity. For example, a pharmaceutical company can use SAP's solutions to monitor its supply chain and identify potential disruptions in the supply of raw materials. By developing alternative sourcing strategies and building inventory buffers, the company can ensure a steady supply of essential medicines, even in the face of disruptions.

Fostering Innovation and Creativity

Innovation and creativity are at the heart of Industry 5.0. By fostering a collaborative relationship between humans and machines, Industry 5.0 enables the development of innovative solutions that address complex challenges. SAP's solutions support businesses in fostering this innovation and creativity.

For instance, an automotive manufacturer can use SAP's intelligent technologies to analyze data on vehicle performance and identify areas for improvement. By collaborating with human experts, the manufacturer can develop innovative solutions that enhance vehicle efficiency, safety, and performance. This not only helps in creating better products but also fosters a culture of innovation and creativity within the organization.

The Human Touch in Industry 5.0

While advanced technologies play a crucial role in Industry 5.0, the human touch remains essential. Industry 5.0 emphasizes the importance of human insights, creativity, and decision-making in the manufacturing process. SAP's human-centric design principles focus on creating user-friendly and intuitive solutions that enable seamless collaboration between humans and machines.

For example, a healthcare provider can use SAP's solutions to analyze patient data and identify trends and patterns. By collaborating with human experts, the provider can develop personalized treatment plans that cater to the unique needs of individual patients. This not only enhances patient outcomes but also fosters a more compassionate and human-centric approach to healthcare.

Conclusion: Embracing the Future of Manufacturing

Industry 5.0 marks a significant evolution in the manufacturing landscape, emphasizing human-machine collaboration, personalization, sustainability, and resilience. SAP, with its comprehensive suite of intelligent technologies and customer-centric solutions, is well-positioned to drive this transformation. By embracing Industry 5.0 principles and leveraging SAP's innovative solutions, businesses can unlock new opportunities, enhance operational efficiency, and create a more sustainable future.

As we step into this new industrial era, the collaboration between humans and machines will redefine manufacturing processes, leading to unprecedented levels of innovation and productivity. With SAP as a transforming force, businesses can navigate the complexities of Industry 5.0 and thrive in this rapidly evolving landscape.

entry.title: Re: Variant update problem with variant management contro...
entry.content: Thank you Nil it worked 😄

entry.title: Re: WEBI report: sum() in footer calculation ignores filters
entry.content:
The problem has been solved.

My universe consists of multiple contexts, and the query I run spans across 2 different flows being merged automatically by BO. One measure requested in the query only belonged to one of those flow/context.

This meant that if in the current block I added that measure (that only belonged to one flow), dimensions/columns corresponding to the other showed no data for lines that did not represent items in the intersection of the 2 flows, yet their values were counted for some reason in the footer summary (sum).

I solved this by not displaying the dimension directly but instead showing a variable that returned the value of the dimension only when the measure belonging only to the other flow was not null:

=[Dimension] WHERE (Not(IsNull([MeasureOfTheOtherFlow])))

Of course this suits my needs, doesn't mean it's ok in any case.

I Also figured out there are settings in web intelligence block display options to show lines with empty measures or with empty dimensions, which helped me figuring out where the problem was.

entry.title: Re: Step-by-Step Migration from SAP BW 7.5 to SAP BW/4HAN...
entry.content:
Hello Amir,

You wrote "Our plan is to continue operating our current BW system alongside BW/4HANA during the migration". For me this leads to the following question: What migration path are you intending to use? InPlace Conversion or Remote Conversion?

My first answer was more for an InPlace Conversion. That means you migrate your objects within the current system (SID remains the same), supported by modes: Compatibility, B4H, R4C. In the end a technical system conversion "upgrades" the system to BW/4. In this approach, DataSources are "switched" to ODP when running the tasklist. The datasource connection is either SAPI or ODP. (=> when using the toolbox for conversion and delta queue cloning)

A remote conversion means a parallel landscape is created and objects are transferred from the existing system to BW/4. This is a totally different approach.

In this approach, objects are transferred to BW/4. On the way of this transfer the objects will be converted. The existing landscape remains nearly unchanged. When DataSources are part of this scope they will be connected as ODP DataSources in BW/4 (incl. delta-queu-cloning to ODP).

As the existing system remains unchanged, the extraction logic (SAPI) will continue in the existing landscape. After the transfer to BW/4 in BW/4 the extraction is done via ODP. So, from a source system point of view there are two targets for the DataSource: SAPI to the "old" system and ODP to BW/4.

Best Regards,

Eduard

entry.title: Re: EML: Read All Instances of a BO
entry.content:
I did this and it gives me no instances as result. Understandable, since no key (there were none) matched any instance:

 

 

DATA empty_table TYPE TABLE FOR READ IMPORT zstore.
READ ENTITIES OF zstore IN LOCAL MODE
ENTITY store
ALL FIELDS WITH CORRESPONDING #( empty_table )
RESULT DATA(result).

entry.title: Re: How to create your own ATC check in ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse
entry.content:
Hi @NitinG ,

table is possible as a parameter type, but the documentation of the table type explicitly says that the table's row type must be elementary:

If you have for example structure as a line type in your table it will not work.

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Use Date input control in a SAC formula to calculate date difference
entry.content:
Hello everyone

I need an help about using a date set in a "Date input control" in a story filter, in a SAC formula

I have this Date input control

I have a model where is present a date dimension called StartingDate

What I need to do is calculate dinamically the day difference between StartingDate and the day set in the date input control.

How can I achieve this?

I tried with a formula calculation of type "Date difference" but I cannot select the date input control.

Anyone can help me?

Thank you so much

Kind regards

entry.title: Re: Websocket connection in SAP DM
entry.content:
Hi Marcel, Manoel,

Thank you for your responses.

I would like to know, similarly to line monitoring POD or Weigh and Dispense POD the recent tag values are reflected on the UI without refreshing the POD.

As observed it looks like Websockets are setup that sends messages as payload to the POD so that a change in asset tag value updates it on the UI immediately.

Is there a way wherein we can setup message subscription to asset tags similar to this in custom POD plugin?

Regards,

Sabahat Shaikh

entry.title: Re: Pick Outbound Delivery - Fiori APP
entry.content:
Hi @cristinavazs,

We can use the the GUI based for the picking process with batch.
If we need to change the GUI app UI, we can use screen persona to create a new UI for this GUI based app.
Hope this helps!!!

Best Regards,
Surya.

entry.title: TM in SAP S/4HANA Public cloud
entry.content: Is there any Transportation management module available in SAP S/4HANA public cloud solution?

entry.title: Re: VPN Speed SAP DC (RISE Base Option)?
entry.content:
Hi,

The VPN port speed in ROT is 1Gbit/s but we offer no SLA on this.

The actual speed you can obtain is less. VPN infrastructure is shared and traffic is using the public internet, so no guarantees.

Best Regards
Henrik Strange

entry.title: Re: Issue with mbt build in Newly Created CAP Project on...
entry.content:
@BhanuKiran1 

Add the following command to mta.yaml. It will generate the gen/db folder.

build-parameters:
  before-all:
    - builder: custom
      commands:
        - npx cds build --production

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite in 2025
entry.content:
Discover how SAP Integration Suite, SAP's premier Enterprise Integration Platform as a Service (EiPaaS), empowers organizations to seamlessly connect and integrate diverse system landscapes. From APIs and processes to data and events, SAP Integration Suite offers robust capabilities designed to streamline your integration journey.

In this session, explore its key features, real-world use cases, and tangible benefits, as well as the latest innovations and an exciting roadmap that positions your business for future success. Whether you're enhancing operational efficiency or driving digital transformation, SAP Integration Suite is your gateway to effortless integration.

Join us and learn how to take your integration strategy to the next level!

Udo Paltzer - VP Product Management SAP Integration Suite SAP SE

Register: https://events.sap.com/sap-btp-2025/en/home

entry.title: I have started the SUM procedure, but I am getting the error "The content couldn't be loaded" Can an
entry.content:
Hi Team I have started SUM while loading its contents in the browser I am getting
"The content couldn't be loaded"
can anyone help me understand why is this error getting?
